narrative: Trump’s weekend threat of 25% tariffs on European autos and stalled US-Iran peace talks dominate the tape, setting up a defensive USD bid for the Tokyo open. EUR/USD closed Friday at 1.1723; the tariff escalation and a +175bp carry disadvantage likely to weigh on the pair at Sunday's open. JPY/USD — G8 Bilateral Theme + risk-premium demand: JPY 1W avg: +1.081% (n=7), USD 1W avg: -0.879% (n=7), G8 spread: 1.960%. While USD/JPY closed Friday at 157.03, geopolitical friction is likely to support JPY outperformance despite the -300bp carry gap. RBA's Tuesday meeting remains the primary TIER-1 focus for AUD/USD (Friday close: 0.7208).
